lead.pitchHook: You have no website at all — just a Facebook page — which means Google can't find you and you're losing customers every day.
lead.notes: Primary web presence is just a Facebook page — no standalone website. High opportunity for a full 1-3 page site build at $2,000.

lead.pitchHook: We help auto shops in Spokane stay on top of their site so the right customers find them — takes the work off your plate for $99/month.
lead.notes: Has a website but not fully evaluated. Assigned trust score 2 from original Excel entry. Needs site audit before calling.

lead.pitchHook: Your site scores a 44 on Google's mobile speed test — that means roughly half your mobile visitors leave before the page even loads.
lead.notes: WordPress site. Mobile PageSpeed score: 44 — very poor. Strong candidate for $99/month performance and maintenance plan.

lead.pitchHook: N/A — denied.
lead.notes: Talked to front desk — denied. WordPress site with pretty good scores, so lower opportunity anyway. Do not contact again.

lead.pitchHook: Your Squarespace site has real performance problems on mobile — we can fix that and take the whole thing off your plate.
lead.notes: Talked to front desk — referred to "Mike" who handles the website. Left a message. Squarespace site, bad performance on mobile and web. FOLLOW UP by 2026-03-23.

lead.pitchHook: Your Google Business Profile is missing a phone number — customers searching for you on Google can't call you directly from the results.
lead.notes: WordPress site with really bad styling. No phone number listed on Google Business Profile — major SEO red flag. Untanglit SEO + copy plan is a great pitch.

lead.pitchHook: Your site has solid performance scores but the design is pushing customers away before they even read what you do.
lead.notes: WordPress site. Good performance stats but bad design — open to a visual redesign pitch. $2,000 site build may be the right conversation. Phone number not found.

lead.pitchHook: Your Squarespace site has poor mobile performance and the design is working against you — we can fix that for $99/month.
lead.notes: Squarespace site with bad mobile performance and bad design. Website URL not yet confirmed — needs lookup before calling.

lead.pitchHook: Your website link is completely broken — anyone who tries to find you online hits a dead end. We can fix that fast.
lead.notes: Web link doesn't even work — dead/broken website. Excellent candidate for a full site build. Lead with "your site is down" opener.

lead.pitchHook: Your site has no clear navigation tabs — visitors can't find what they're looking for and leave. That's fixable in an afternoon.
lead.notes: WordPress site with okay performance but no tab navigation (hard to navigate). No phone number on Google Business Profile. SEO and UX fixes are easy upsell for $99/month plan.

lead.pitchHook: On mobile, your phone number and contact form are buried — most people searching for a contractor give up before they find how to call you.
lead.notes: Squarespace site. Hard to find contact info and bad mobile experience. Pitch making contact info instantly visible on mobile as the core value prop.

lead.pitchHook: Your website shows almost no content — just three broken buttons and a blank page — so anyone who searches for you leaves immediately.
lead.notes: Wix site. Nearly zero content visible on homepage — only 3 empty nav buttons render. No menu, no photos, no hours, no about section. Uses personal Gmail as business email (maplestreetbistro@gmail.com). Mobile likely scores ~45. Strong full-build pitch: 1-3 page site at $2,000.

lead.pitchHook: Your site has a "Powered by SpotHopper" watermark on every page — it looks like a template, not your brand.
lead.notes: SpotHopper restaurant marketing template. "Powered by SpotHopper" watermark visible. Basic info only — hours, location, phone. No differentiation from other SpotHopper cafe sites. Contact is Paul (paul@remiscafe.com). Pitch: custom branding and more compelling presentation.

lead.pitchHook: Your SpotHopper template does the basics, but a custom site would let your 25-year reputation and awards actually show up in Google searches.
lead.notes: SpotHopper template but well-populated — full menus, specials, happy hours. In business 25+ years, award-winning. Lower priority. Phone not listed on front page. Pitch would need to be about SEO and brand differentiation.

lead.pitchHook: Your website was built in the early 2000s and doesn't work on phones — you have great customer reviews buried in a layout most people will leave in 5 seconds.
lead.notes: Old-style static HTML/CSS site with early-2000s design aesthetic. No mobile responsiveness. No online booking. In business since 1984 with excellent reviews — the content is there but the presentation is killing conversions. Located in Spokane Valley.

lead.pitchHook: Your site was built on a generic builder that locks you in — we can make it yours and keep it fresh for $99/month.
lead.notes: Built by Consumr Buzz (basic local site builder). Generic template with no real differentiation or personality. Has phone prominently but no online scheduling. $99/month maintenance pitch for content freshness and SEO.

lead.pitchHook: The "Years of Experience" counter on your homepage is broken — it shows "0+" to every visitor right now.
lead.notes: WordPress/generic template. Animated counters for "Years Experience" and "Customer Satisfaction" are broken — showing "0+" on live site. Very sparse copy, no real project photos loaded in gallery. Uses Gmail (constructionvalleyllc@gmail.com). Strong opener: broken counter is visible and embarrassing.

lead.pitchHook: Your website still says copyright 2018 — it's been 7 years since it was updated and it shows the moment someone visits on their phone.
lead.notes: Wix site. Footer shows "Share by:" (Wix artifact) and copyright 2018 — 7 years outdated. No online estimate form. Very thin content despite 30+ years in business. Mobile PageSpeed likely 40-55. Business email found: info@alpineremodelers.com.

lead.pitchHook: Your construction site has a large section for a religious devotional newsletter that seems out of place — it may be confusing potential clients.
lead.notes: Serves Spokane County and Kootenai County (ID). Idaho area code. Has an embedded section for "Lee Arnold's Daily Dose of Boldness" religious newsletter — off-brand and confusing on a construction company site. Decent structure otherwise. $99/month cleanup and content maintenance pitch.

lead.pitchHook: Your website is just a paragraph of text with a contact form — no photos, no service menu, no booking link — and that's what potential clients see before deciding to call.
lead.notes: Barely a website — single page of plain text with no photos, no booking system, no service menu with prices, no before/after gallery. A proper 3-page site at $2,000 would transform their booking volume immediately. Pitch visual credibility hard.

lead.pitchHook: Your site shows a "No menu assigned!" error to every single visitor right now — your navigation is broken on the live site.
lead.notes: WordPress site. "No menu assigned!" navigation error is visible on the live site — broken nav. Extremely sparse content otherwise. Claims to be "Spokane's #1 Best Hair Salon" but the site immediately contradicts that. Very strong pitch: lead with the live error.

lead.business: Aspen Creek Hair Salon and Spa
lead.pitchHook: Your Wix site works okay but booth renter pages and service menus go stale fast — $99/month keeps it all current without you lifting a finger.
lead.notes: Wix site ("top of page" / "bottom of page" footer artifacts). Decent content but outdated look. Booth rent model — multiple independent stylists, so decision-maker is Misty (owner). Ask for Misty when calling. Pitch $99/month to keep stylist pages and service menu current.

lead.pitchHook: Your website has multiple spelling errors in the very first paragraph visitors read, and your contact button links to a broken page on a different domain.
lead.notes: WordPress site still hosted on .wpengine.com staging domain — not on a custom URL. Multiple visible spelling errors in hero copy: "synertystically colline", "atmosthpere", "acheive". Contact button links to thehealinghut.com (different domain) — broken. Owner is Loni Hall. Instagram is active. Strong credentials (Gonzaga president testimonial) buried in bad site.

lead.pitchHook: Your product shop section is live on your site right now showing "Coming Soon!" — it looks unfinished to every visitor.
lead.notes: WordPress site. Solo stylist: Jaime Lang. "Coming Soon!" product shop section is unfinished and publicly visible. Blog posts exist but are generic. Booking handled through Gloss Genius (external). Pitch $99/month to finish the shop section and keep content current. Located in Spokane Valley.

lead.pitchHook: Your contact page is returning a 404 error right now — anyone who clicks "Contact" on your site hits a dead end and gives up.
lead.notes: Wix site. Contact page returns 404 — active broken link costing leads. Booking via The Customer Factor (external third-party link). Phone not listed on website. Founded by Kevin Johnson (from About page). Located in Spokane Valley.

lead.pitchHook: Your seasonal services — spring cleanup, snow removal — need to be front and center at the right time of year. We do that automatically for $99/month.
lead.notes: Wix site. Very minimal content, no differentiation. Contact email: brittany@rondavislawncare.com. Has client login portal (LawnGateway). Pitch $99/month to keep seasonal content (spring cleanup, snow removal) timely and visible.

lead.pitchHook: Your contact page links are routing through the Wayback Machine archive — patients trying to book an appointment are hitting a broken page.
lead.notes: WordPress site. Contact page links route through web.archive.org (Wayback Machine) — site or key pages appear broken or cached from years ago. Old WordPress theme. No online booking — just a form that emails staff. Owner is Dr. Joe Luchini. Email: info@luchinidds.com. Very strong pitch: patients literally cannot contact them.

lead.pitchHook: Your publicly listed business email is a personal Yahoo address from 2003 — it makes it hard for clients to trust they're contacting a real business.
lead.notes: Basic website builder template. Contact email is tampoodle2003@yahoo.com — a personal Yahoo address, highly unprofessional. In operation 50+ years. Owner/operator is Tami Richardson. No pricing listed. Pitch: professional email + pricing page for $99/month.

lead.pitchHook: Your site still has a GoDaddy placeholder email address visible in the page source — the template was never fully set up.
lead.notes: GoDaddy website builder. Default placeholder "filler@godaddy.com" is visible in page source — template was never fully cleaned up. Minimal homepage content. No address listed. Opened September 2020. Pitch: "your site still has GoDaddy placeholder code in it."

lead.pitchHook: Your "website" is hosted on a third-party food ordering domain — anyone Googling your business name just sees a generic ordering page, not your brand.
lead.notes: Their web presence is a page on the .com-fnb.com food ordering platform — not an owned domain or real website. Located at 4237 S Cheney Spokane Rd. Features live music, art, and a distinctive menu (bison meatloaf, pumpkin bread french toast). Strong brand story with no web home to tell it. Pitch: own your online presence for $2,000.

lead.pitchHook: Your website is returning a "503 Service Unavailable" error — it is completely down right now and anyone searching for you online can't find you.
lead.notes: Website honeywoncafe.com returned a 503 Service Unavailable error at time of research — site is down. Fast casual Korean-fusion cafe at 1330 N Washington St. Open 7am-3pm. Strong pitch: "your site is down and you're losing customers." Build from scratch.

lead.pitchHook: Your entire website is four short paragraphs and a phone number — there's nothing there that would convince a property manager or contractor to choose you over a competitor.
lead.notes: Extremely minimal website built by Night Fox Digital — just WHO ARE WE, SERVICES, LEGAL INFO, and CONTACT US sections with a total of ~8 sentences. No photos, no project portfolio, no case studies. Focuses on commercial/industrial/institutional HVAC. Owner/contact is Shawn (shawn@stlhead.com). Located at 4103 E Joseph, Spokane. Fax: 509.922.4257.

lead.pitchHook: Your pricing page and class schedule need constant updates — that's exactly what the $99/month plan handles so you can focus on coaching.
lead.notes: Unique altitude training studio offering cross-training and yoga at simulated elevations up to 20,000 ft. Website has decent content but pricing and class schedules change frequently. Phone not listed on site. Pitch $99/month to keep memberships, pricing, and class info current. Lower priority — site is functional.

lead.pitchHook: Your class schedule, pricing, and free trial offer need to stay current — $99/month takes that off your plate so you can focus on coaching.

Multiple class types: fundamentals, fighter, kids. Functional website but maintenance pitch is solid for class schedule and pricing updates.

lead.pitchHook: Your website still loads over HTTP instead of HTTPS — browsers like Chrome and Safari show visitors a "Not Secure" warning before they even see your work.
lead.notes: WordPress site still on http:// (not https) — a security red flag that Chrome flags with a "Not Secure" badge. Established 2007, in Kendall Yards since 2014. Only APP-member shop in Eastern WA — great credential buried on a dated site. Email: info@momstattoo.com. Strong credentials deserve better presentation. Booking via Acuity Scheduling.

lead.pitchHook: Your site has a Facebook Messenger chat widget but no phone number anywhere — clients have to DM to book rather than just calling.

Owner appears to be Teresa based on reviews. 10+ years experience. Pitch: add click-to-call and improve mobile booking flow for $99/month.

lead.pitchHook: Your business contact email is a personal Gmail address — clients and vendors may question whether it's a legitimate business.
lead.notes: Basic template nail salon at 10208 N Division St #102, Spokane. Uses foxynguyen@gmail.com as contact email — personal Gmail. lead.pitchHook: Your site was slow to load during research — page speed issues are the number one reason mobile visitors leave before booking.

lead.pitchHook: Your site's booking button just links to a third-party Booksy page — a $99/month plan keeps your own site looking sharp and up to date.

lead.pitchHook: Your site has two locations but no phone number listed — walk-in customers who look you up online have no way to call ahead and check the wait.

lead.pitchHook: Florists live and die by seasonal content — Valentine's Day, Mother's Day, weddings — and $99/month keeps yours timely without you lifting a finger.

lead.pitchHook: Your bouquet photos and seasonal offerings need to rotate constantly — that's exactly what $99/month handles automatically.

lead.pitchHook: Your website uses a table-based HTML layout from the early 2000s — it doesn't resize on phones at all and likely shows up as a tiny unreadable page to half your visitors.

Services: family portraits, senior portraits, business portraits, pet portraits, photo restoration. Strong story, terrible site. Pitch a modern 3-4 page build. Text/call Mike at (509) 995-4881.

lead.pitchHook: Your seasonal services — spring window cleaning, Christmas lights, gutter cleaning — need to be front and center at the right time of year; $99/month keeps that rotating automatically.

lead.pitchHook: Your entire website is just an Instagram feed — there's no menu, no specials, no real page for Google to index, so customers searching online can't find what you offer.

lead.pitchHook: Your "Lunch & Dinner" menu button links to "/undefined" — it's a broken link that every customer who visits your site hits when they try to see your food menu.

lead.pitchHook: Your "Beer on Tap" link on the homepage goes back to the homepage — customers clicking to see your 24 draft beers hit a dead end and never see the tap list.

lead.pitchHook: Your blog has only 3 posts — the most recent from November 2025 — and your upcoming class schedule shows a January start date that's now months out of date.

lead.pitchHook: Your pricing, classes, and store are spread across three different websites — patients have to bounce between gaitwaychiropractic.com, spokanewellness.com, and Square to find information.

lead.pitchHook: Your blog has three posts — the newest from March 2024 — which means patients searching for eye care info find year-old content and may trust a competitor who posts regularly.

Perfect $99/month subscription: monthly blog post + seasonal promotions (back to school, sports seasons, senior care). Phone confirmed (509) 327-9505.

lead.pitchHook: Your homepage says "16+ years of experience" in one place and "over 5 years" in another — the contradiction makes it look unprofessional to potential clients doing their research.
lead.notes: ACTIVE — family-owned, licensed bonded insured. WordPress site. Copy inconsistency: "16+ Years of Experience" headline vs. "over 5 years" body copy — same page. Project gallery mixes stock photos with real work photos. Services: door/window install, painting, kitchen/bath remodel, flooring, fencing. $99/month pitch: fix copy, swap stock photos for real work photos, keep seasonal services (spring exterior work, winter repairs) current.

lead.pitchHook: Your homepage has a visible typo ("Saftey") in a section header, and your business contact email is a personal Gmail — small things that erode trust when clients are comparing contractors.

lead.pitchHook: Your website opens to a blank splash page with just an "Enter site" button — on mobile, many visitors give up before they ever see your classes or schedule.
lead.notes: UNVERIFIED ACTIVE STATUS — site is an http:// splash screen with no real content; confirm business is still operating before calling. Offers yoga, aerial arts, and bellydance. Website still on http:// (not https) — browsers flag it. Homepage is a full-screen splash with just "Enter site" — extremely high bounce rate on mobile. Class schedules for aerial and dance change every session. Phone not listed on site. Needs: https upgrade, mobile-friendly landing, and regular class schedule updates — natural $99/month subscription. Phone needs lookup.

lead.pitchHook: You're already blogging — let us handle the writing so you can focus on clients instead of content. $99/month and you never touch the website again.
lead.notes: ACTIVE — blog posts in February and March 2026 (very recent). Established 1996, located at 103 E Sprague Ave Ste 102, Spokane. WordPress site with active blog. Site is reasonably well-maintained but runs on a busy agent team — the $99/month "we write and post your content" angle is the pitch here, not fixing broken things. lead.pitchHook: Your price list is hard to find and your site has no way to book online — customers who find you on Google and can't immediately book just go to the next detailer.

lead.pitchHook: Your homepage literally shows "No Blog Posts found" in big text — it's a placeholder from an unfinished WordPress template that every visitor sees when they land on your site.

lead.pitchHook: You have 8 artists who each finish new work constantly — but your blog hasn't been updated since February 2024 and most of their portfolios are static snapshots that don't reflect what they're doing now.

lead.pitchHook: Your 9 barbers each book through a different platform — Square, The Cut, or their own links — and none of your service prices are listed, so every new customer has to call just to know what a haircut costs.

lead.pitchHook: Your website is entirely plain text with no photos, no logo, and no visual design — it reads like a flyer posted on a corkboard, which makes it hard to trust you with a yard that costs hundreds of dollars to maintain.

lead.pitchHook: Your homepage is just your hours and address — customers visiting your site find no specials, no seasonal drinks, no upcoming events, nothing to share with friends or convince them to come in.

lead.pitchHook: Your entire website is a studio name, two artist links, and a copyright line — there is no portfolio, no booking, no address, and no phone number for potential clients to find you.

lead.pitchHook: Your homepage has a broken Facebook feed taking up half the page — visitors on mobile see a giant blank section where your social content should appear, making the site look unfinished.
